sprayer_output_verification.objective: Confirm that actual sprayer output matches the intended carrier volume and rate.
key_steps: Check nozzle condition and spacing.
Measure travel speed under realistic field conditions.
Capture nozzle output for a timed interval and compare across the boom.
Calculate gallons per acre and compare to target output.
common_failures: using assumed speed instead of measured speed
mixed nozzle wear across the boom
calibrating on flat pavement but spraying on uneven turf

sprayer_mixing_sequence_workflow.objective: Reduce incompatibility, precipitation, and rate mistakes during tank mixing.
when_to_use: wettable powder or suspension concentrate mixes
key_steps: Start with clean water and agitation.
Add products in a consistent compatibility-aware sequence.
Use jar tests when combinations are unfamiliar or risky.
Confirm final tank volume and rate math before leaving the fill station.
common_failures: adding products in a random order
mixing chemistry that should have been separated

spreader_pattern_testing.objective: Verify even granular distribution and realistic swath width.
key_steps: Use trays or collection pans across the spread pattern.
Run realistic passes at measured speed.
Compare left-right symmetry and center loading.
Set overlap and swath width from actual pattern data.
common_failures: using manufacturer width without field testing

granular_rate_per_1000_conversion.objective: Convert label or program rates into practical spreader targets without math errors.
when_to_use: switching between per-acre and per-1000 metrics
key_steps: Convert label rate into the working unit before loading.
Measure test area accurately.
Weigh product used on the test area and compare to target.
Adjust gate setting and speed only after confirming the math.
common_failures: confusing product weight with nutrient rate
rounding too aggressively on small areas
testing on an area that was not measured accurately

travel_speed_checkpoint_workflow.objective: Keep application rate calculations anchored to real operating speed.
key_steps: Mark a representative course over the same terrain type to be treated.
Time repeated passes in the actual operating gear and throttle range.
Use the average speed for calculations, not the best run.
Recheck speed when product load, terrain, or operator changes materially.
common_failures: using road speed instead of turf speed

recordkeeping_and_post_application_revie.topic: Recordkeeping and post application review calibration
recordkeeping_and_post_application_revie.objective: Use records to catch calibration drift, pattern issues, and operator inconsistency over time.
when_to_use: after any miss or injury event
key_steps: Record rate target, area treated, product used, and operator details.
Compare expected versus actual product consumption.
Investigate discrepancies before the next application cycle.
Link any visible miss, streak, or injury back to calibration records.
common_failures: keeping records too vague to audit
treating calibration as separate from performance review
